provenance.source: docs/plans/2026-06-12-metadata-rebuild-stage1-cooking-skills-ingredients-worksheet-decided.md (worksheet fold-in map — PRIMARY) + docs/plans/c02-session1-discovery/q1-vocab-census.md §3/§4c (kebab/case twins + named §5 remaps)
provenance.task: C02 P1.1 — deterministic alias-floor (the ~92-94% deterministic core)

representation.aliasMap: raw census source-value → canonical Title-Case value (a member of c02-vocab.json's closed set). A FOLD: the source maps to exactly ONE canonical value with NO lesson-context needed.
representation.drops: raw census source-values dropped at re-tag (cosmetic/craft/composite noise + B-lite pantry Salt/Oil/Soy-sauce). A DROP is distinguishable from a fold and is NOT a canonical value. The LLM/floor removes these; they never become a stored tag.

discipline: Vague LLM-judgment tags are NOT folded here (the LLM replaces them by reading the lesson): Basic Skills, Cooking Techniques, Stovetop cooking, Various techniques, Cooking, Heating. Also left to the LLM: the multi-group words Fruits/Vegetables (span many groups), Herbs (Fresh herbs vs Spices), and the EXACT bare group names Spices/Dairy (left so the full re-read can upgrade to a specific). By contrast, NEAR-SYNONYMS of a single group ARE floored to that group: Legumes→Beans & legumes, Citrus→Citrus fruits, Dried fruit→Dried fruits, Seeds/Nuts→Nuts & seeds, Various spices→Spices. This floor-vs-LLM boundary is provisional and pilot-tuned (design §3).

IDEMPOTENCY PRECONDITION (P1.3): no canonical value (cooking ∪ groups ∪ specifics) is an aliasMap KEY — canonical values map to themselves implicitly. So 'Measuring', 'Baking', 'Alliums', 'Chickpeas', 'Citrus fruits', 'Oranges', 'Lime', 'Apples', 'Coconut' are absent as keys.
Every aliasMap VALUE is a member of c02-vocab.json's canonical set.
Pantry B-lite: Sugar (and its sweetener kin) → 'Sweeteners' group; Salt/Oil/Soy sauce → drops.

MATCHING IS CASE-INSENSITIVE + TRIM (normalize.ts matchKey = NFC + trim + toLowerCase). Keys are matched on their lowercased+trimmed form, so case twins (Mixing/mixing, Chopping/chopping, Beets/beets, Alliums/alliums, …) need NO explicit lowercase/uppercase duplicate key. Additionally, every canonical VALUE is loaded into the per-field match lookup at its own matchKey (the 'canonical-case rule'), so a correctly-spelled-but-miscased canonical (e.g. 'beets'→'Beets', 'tahini'→'Tahini') normalizes to canonical casing for free. Plurals/synonyms are NOT caught by case-folding and DO need explicit keys (Avocados→Avocado, Sun butter/Sunbutter→Sunflower butter, Limes/Lemons→Lime/Lemon, Cucumber/Carrot→Cucumbers/Carrots). The loader THROWS at load time if two match-keys collide onto different canonicals (verified zero collisions on the real data).

P2.1b FLOOR TOP-UP (2026-06-23): a live-PROD coverage audit added 14 clean single-target ingredient folds the alias map missed because the original census was accidentally run against TEST (PROD-only values were absent). All are unambiguous (no lesson context needed): Beans/Peas→Beans & legumes, Squash→Squash, cucumbers & melons, Parmesan cheese/Mozzarella cheese→Cheese, Sour Cream/Buttermilk/Condensed milk→Dairy, Various seeds→Nuts & seeds, Beyond Sausage (pea protein)→Tofu & plant proteins (distinct from the existing bare 'Beyond Sausage' key — different matchKey, same target), Lettuce→Leafy greens, Cereal/grains & Whole wheat wraps→Grains & starches, Beet juice→Beets. The vague/ambiguous cooking_skills residual and the 'Herbs & Aromatics'/'herbs-aromatics' catch-all are DELIBERATELY left to the LLM, not floored here.
